what_it_does: This file is a terrain-entry ledger record that describes the ~/.vi shared-context directory as a cross-session markdown instruction frame auto-loaded into Claude Code sessions, listing its observed files, stated purpose, wiring to the Company channel fabric, and evidence disclaimers.
observations: Frontmatter labels it type=terrain-entry, register=descriptive, kind=config, status=unconfirmed, with coverage 3 of 31 files read.
It cites CLAUDE.md as naming a four-layer architecture frame: DNA, FACTORY, GALLERY, and COMPANY as the substrate.
It notes rules/cross-session-channels.md documents fabric session launches via ~/company/channels/ using an MCP server and channel handle registry.
It relates to [[company]] and [[company-systemd]] through the channel fabric.
It explicitly marks most files as not read in full and distinguishes evidence from interpretation.
standouts: The directory is described as auto-loaded into every Claude Code session as active instructions.
It documents cross-session channel launch wiring in ~/company/channels/.
Status is unconfirmed despite being presented as a descriptive, canonical-seeming frame entry.
Coverage is low relative to the total file count.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with typed ledger fields (type, register, kind, status, coverage, relates-to, tags).
Uses wiki-style [[...]] links for related entries.
Follows a structured section layout: What it is, How it works, What it connects to, When/where, Notes/evidence.
Includes explicit evidence/observed vs not-read disclaimers.
concerns: Status is unconfirmed while the entry describes a canonical frame.
Only 3 of 31 files have been read, so most content is unverified.
Some phrasing blends observed evidence with interpretive framing (e.g., 'substrate all layers resolve through').
notes: A briefing entry that maps ~/.vi as a session-level instruction frame and traces its observed connection to the Company channel fabric.
questions: What is the exact technical mechanism that auto-loads ~/.vi into every Claude Code session?
What do the unread files (IDENTITY.md, PROJECTS.md, REGISTRY.md, TELESCOPE_*.md, TIM.md) actually contain?
What is the precise meaning of 'fabric' in this context?
How direct is the relationship to [[company-systemd]] beyond the indirect channel reference?
Why is the entry status 'unconfirmed' if it is described as a stable frame?
intention_signals: To map ~/.vi as a stable, reusable orientation layer so sessions can locate the shared instruction set and understand how it connects to the Company channel launch mechanism.
novelty: Treats a local dotfile directory as a first-class ledger terrain-entry with coverage tracking, evidence annotations, and explicit not-read disclaimers.
contribution: Records the existence, stated purpose, observed contents, and relational wiring of the ~/.vi shared-context directory, preserving the fact that it is used as session-level active instructions and is linked to the Company channel launch mechanism.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain entry for the ~/.vi shared-context directory, describing it as a cross-session markdown instruction frame auto-loaded into Claude Code sessions and documenting its observed files, described architecture layers, and connection to the Company channel fabric.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and orient future work to the ~/.vi instruction set and its role in launching cross-session Company channels.
